item.title: Jamie Dimon warns private credit losses will be larger than feared
item.description: JPMorgan chief raises alarm on weakening lending standards in annual shareholder letter

item.title: Fast fashion’s offcuts are fighting back
item.description: Squeezing money out of a legacy brand may be better than trying to build a new one

item.title: FirstFT: EU warns of excessive energy support measures
item.description: Also in today’s newsletter: Private equity buyouts and Ukrainian drone campaign

item.title: Trump family-linked investment group backs spa company offering medical screening
item.description: XWell plans to roll out AI-powered infectious disease tool at airports during World Cup

item.title: Russian crypto payment system expands into Africa
item.description: Videos show A7 office in Nigeria, while company also claims new branch in Zimbabwe

item.title: Stefano Pessina vows to rebuild ‘all the value’ lost at his Boots empire
item.description: Italian billionaire hails his wife’s turnaround of the pharmacy chain in his first interview since its struggling US owner was taken private

item.title: Private equity buyouts slump as AI fears and war dent dealmaking
item.description: Groups agreed acquisitions worth $172bn in three months to March, a 36 per cent fall from previous quarter

item.title: Power-starved Cuba deepens reliance on Chinese solar tech
item.description: Imports of solar technology have soared, offering some respite for country that now faces near-total oil blockade

item.title: AI has arrived in auditing. Are regulators ready?
item.description: The technology is already leading to rapid changes in the way company accounts are reviewed

item.title: Ukraine’s drones dent Russia’s war-fuelled oil windfall
item.description: Disrupted exports add pressure to markets already reeling from Iran conflict

item.title: China and Europe launch rare joint space mission
item.description: Solar-focused project seeks to understand geomagnetic storms that can disrupt terrestrial communications

item.title: Deutsche Bank chief earns bumper share returns as rebound bet pays off
item.description: Christian Sewing holds stock worth €15mn and another €25mn in unvested shares after turnaround fuels share price rally

item.title: Chinese micro-dramas target US with familiar tropes for phone audience
item.description: Love and fantasy stories dominate a wildly popular genre in China that is trying to adapt to western tastes

item.title: Let technology explore what the voters really want
item.description: New insights into motivation and strength of feeling could help democracy work better

item.title: World’s biggest battery maker takes ambitions to the high seas
item.description: China’s CATL wants to electrify global shipping fleets but hurdles remain to large-scale adoption

item.title: Neurocrine nears $2.5bn-plus deal for biotech behind obesity disorder treatment
item.description: Soleno Therapeutics makes first commercialised drug for extreme hunger caused by Prader-Willi syndrome

item.title: AI dolls offer companionship to the elderly
item.description: South Korea’s strained social care system turns to ChatGPT-enabled devices as population ages

item.title: Is AI the new fracking?
item.description: The backlash against data centres chimes with energy Nimbyism of the past

item.title: How Trump became tech’s regulator-in-chief
item.description: His interventions in the sector exceed anything the EU has done

item.title: ‘Lights are blinking’: LaGuardia crash raises alarm over US aviation safety
item.description: Fatal Air Canada Express collision highlights years of under-investment in ageing air traffic control system

item.title: All that glisters: Maga influencers promote gold but investors feel short-changed
item.description: Marketing pitches tell retirees the precious metal is a safe harbour amid claims of misrepresentation and exorbitant fees

item.title: Nelson Peltz’s bidding war highlights $25bn wave of asset manager consolidation
item.description: Quest for scale puts global money manager tie-ups on pace to crush last year’s deal total as costs and competition mount

item.title: US rescues second airman from fighter jet shot down in Iran
item.description: Recovery follows combat search and rescue mission

item.title: Stagflation fears demolish confidence in UK housing
item.description: Shares across the sector are taking a beating

item.title: Military briefing: How Iran keeps firing missiles under bombardment
item.description: Tehran is still launching retaliatory strikes on Israel and Gulf states

item.title: 400% gains for AI stocks help drive Hong Kong IPOs to 5-year high
item.description: Deal backlogs and stricter quality controls are pushing some tech firms back to mainland Chinese listings

item.title: Pharma just ‘scratching the surface’ of weight-loss drug market
item.description: Novo Nordisk chief estimates industry reaching only 15% of potential customers at most

item.title: The professional negotiators who do deals with cyber criminals
item.description: With ransomware attacks on the rise, businesses are calling on a new class of security expert to help with high-stakes talks

item.title: UK courts Anthropic to expand in London after US defence clash
item.description: Keir Starmer’s government steps up efforts to get American AI start-up to grow its presence in Britain

item.title: Renters’ Rights Act brings big changes to UK property market
item.description: New rules aim to provide safety and security for tenants, but landlords are anxious
